title: Ordered-CatBoost specialist on strategy state tuples
summary: CatBoost was designed to reduce prediction shift from categorical target statistics, which is directly relevant here because `Driver`, `Race`, `Year`, `Compound`, and `Stint` are high-cardinality and strategy-like.
rationale: CatBoost was designed to reduce prediction shift from categorical target statistics, which is directly relevant here because `Driver`, `Race`, `Year`, `Compound`, and `Stint` are high-cardinality and strategy-like. The current best solutions mostly use raw categories, frequencies, and manual heuristics, not leakage-safe online CTRs over state tuples.
implementation_hint: Train a separate CatBoost model with categorical inputs left raw and combinations such as `Driver x Compound`, `Race x Compound`, `Driver x Race_Year`, `Stint x Compound`, `PriorPitCount x Compound`. Evaluate only with `Race_Year`/race-grouped OOF splits and blend its OOF predictions with the current GBDT stack.
expected_effect: Captures stable but nonlinear strategy priors and interaction effects that manual frequency features miss, with a realistic chance of small additive AUC gain.
risk: Can overfit historical driver/team behavior if folds are not race-blocked; year drift may make some priors brittle.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
